raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'improve this draft' refer to editing for style, flow, and structure only, or does it include reviewing content such as logical consistency and evidence alignment?","rationale":{"R1.2":"Improving the draft centrally requires checking the logical consistency of the proposed method, results, and motivations to ensure a coherent argument.","R1.3":"The delegation expects alignment between the claimed improvements in win-rate and reward hacking reduction with the reported experiments and datasets.","R1.5":"Structural reorganization at the section or paragraph level is central to enhancing the narrative arc of the draft's description of the method and its implications.","R1.6":"Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is load-bearing for any draft improvement to achieve better grammar, flow, and conciseness.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is centrally important to highlight any assumptions or limitations in the contamination classifier and theorem in the improved draft.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance ensures that improvements do not exaggerate the gains or the applicability of ContamRLHF beyond the evidence provided.","RX.3":"Scope adherence maintains the focus on the reward-model training procedure without expanding into unrelated areas during improvements.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is key to prevent any enhancements that could inadvertently promote misuse of RLHF in AI alignment contexts.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability ensure that citations and data sources in the draft are properly attributed and verifiable in the improvement process."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'improve this draft' refer to editing for style, flow, and structure only, or does it include reviewing content such as logical consistency and evidence alignment?

rationale.R1.2: Improving the draft centrally requires checking the logical consistency of the proposed method, results, and motivations to ensure a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation expects alignment between the claimed improvements in win-rate and reward hacking reduction with the reported experiments and datasets.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization at the section or paragraph level is central to enhancing the narrative arc of the draft's description of the method and its implications.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is load-bearing for any draft improvement to achieve better grammar, flow, and conciseness.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is centrally important to highlight any assumptions or limitations in the contamination classifier and theorem in the improved draft.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance ensures that improvements do not exaggerate the gains or the applicability of ContamRLHF beyond the evidence provided.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence maintains the focus on the reward-model training procedure without expanding into unrelated areas during improvements.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is key to prevent any enhancements that could inadvertently promote misuse of RLHF in AI alignment contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability ensure that citations and data sources in the draft are properly attributed and verifiable in the improvement process.
